reasoning: VICI closed at 29.25 on Friday, April 11, 2025 (the most recent trading day prior to April 13). Short-term, the stock has rebounded sharply from a low of 27.70 earlier in the week, showing positive momentum that slightly favors an increase over the next few trading days (P=0.53 for April 20). As a REIT, VICI pays a substantial dividend (approx. 6% annually). Because standard price charts do not adjust for dividends, the stock's expected price appreciation is lower than its expected total return and may be close to flat, as regular dividend payouts mechanics reduce the share price on ex-dividend dates. Consequently, as the horizon extends and crosses ex-dividend payouts (likely in June and September), the expected price drift will be dragged down by these payouts while variance increases. This naturally pushes longer-term probabilities toward a pure coin flip. Thus, the probabilities taper smoothly to 0.50 at the 6-month mark.
